title: Desarmadero Operations Prototype
description: Turned one discovery call with an auto-dismantling yard into a clickable operations prototype, PRD, and functional spec before quoting the full build.
seoTitle: Desarmadero Operations Prototype | Forward-Deployed Product Engineering Case Study
seoDescription: A forward-deployed product engineering case study: one discovery call became a SvelteKit prototype, PRD, spec, and live demo for an auto-dismantling yard operations system.
constraints: The client knew the business workflow, but the operating model was spread across paper budget sheets, WhatsApp, Excel, and memory.
The prototype needed to prove understanding before scope and pricing decisions, not pretend to be the full production system.
problem: A returning client had one discovery call, paper budget sheets, WhatsApp, Excel, memory, and a 301-position yard that needed to become an operating system.
solution: Mapped the real workflow from a discovery call, wrote the PRD and functional spec, then built a role-based prototype the client could click through before committing to the full scope.
result: The client could validate the operating model through a working app instead of abstract requirements.
The prototype translated messy spoken input into a structured product plan with a live demo, PRD, functional spec, and future phases.
The work created a public forward-deployed engineering sample: discovery artifacts on one side, a clickable operations system on the other.
deliveryHighlights: A live role-based prototype, seeded demo, discovery transcript, PRD, functional spec, and prototype screenshots.
The prototype covers counter sales, cash-desk release, dismantle assignment, yard-map search, and a mobile dismantler view.
Five roles were modeled with separate surfaces: administrator, seller, cash desk, dismantle manager, and dismantler.
